sections.paragraph: <entryFree id="n28612" key="dus" type="main" opt="n" TEIform="entryFree"> <orth extent="pref" lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="orth">δῠσ-</orth>, insepar. Prefix, opp. <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">εὖ</foreign>, <sense id="n28612.0" n="A" level="1" opt="n" TEIform="sense"> <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">un-, mis</tr_>-, with notion of <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">hard, bad, unlucky</tr_>, etc., as <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">δυσήλιος, δύσαγνος</foreign>; <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">destroying the good sense</tr_> of a word, or <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">increasing its bad sense</tr_>: hence, joined even to words expressing negation, as <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">δυσάμμορος, δυσανάσχετος</foreign>; poet. in strong contrasts, as <foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">Πάρις Δύσπαρις, γάμος δύσγαμος</foreign>. Before <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">στ</itype>, <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">σθ</itype>, <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">σπ</itype>, <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">σφ</itype><foreign lang="greek" TEIform="foreign">, σχ</foreign>, the final <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">ς</itype> was omitted, <abbr TEIform="abbr">v.</abbr> <itype lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="itype">δυστ</itype>-. (Cf. Skt. <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">du[snull ]-, dur</tr_>-, e.g. <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">durmanās</tr_>, <xr opt="n" TEIform="xr"> <lbl opt="n" TEIform="lbl">=</lbl> <ref targOrder="U" lang="greek" TEIform="ref">δυσμενής</ref> </xr>; ONorse <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">tor</tr_>-, e.g. <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">torsóttligr</tr_> (<etym lang="greek" opt="n" TEIform="etym">δύσμαχος</etym>); OIr. <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">du-, do</tr_>-, e.g. <tr_ opt="n" TEIform="tr">dochruth</tr_> 'misshapen'.)</sense> </entryFree>
